is a town and [municipality](/wiki/Municipalities_of_Colombia "Municipalities of Colombia") in the [Colombian](/wiki/Colombia "Colombia") [department](/wiki/Departments_of_Colombia "Departments of Colombia") of [Antioquia](/wiki/Antioquia_Department "Antioquia Department"). In 2020, the population was estimated to be 52,129. It is part of the subregion of [Eastern Antioquia](/wiki/Eastern_Antioquia "Eastern Antioquia"). It is situated 24 km east of [Medellín](/wiki/Medell%C3%ADn "Medellín").

["Inicio"](http://www.guarne-antioquia.gov.co/nuestromunicipio.shtml?apc=m1I1--&m=f#identificacion).`{{cite web}}`: CS1 maint: deprecated archival service ([link](/wiki/Category:CS1_maint:_deprecated_archival_service "Category:CS1 maint: deprecated archival service"))

**Guarne** is a town and [municipality](/wiki/Municipalities_of_Colombia "Municipalities of Colombia") in the [Colombian](/wiki/Colombia "Colombia") [department](/wiki/Departments_of_Colombia "Departments of Colombia") of [Antioquia](/wiki/Antioquia_Department "Antioquia Department"). In 2020, the population was estimated to be 52,129. It is part of the subregion of [Eastern Antioquia](/wiki/Eastern_Antioquia "Eastern Antioquia"). It is situated 24 km east of [Medellín](/wiki/Medell%C3%ADn "Medellín").

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Guarne&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

The first settlers of Guarne were [Tahamíes Indians](/wiki/Taham%C3%ADes_Indians "Tahamíes Indians") who came from the [Nare River](/wiki/Nare_River "Nare River"). In 1541, [Alvaro Mendoza](/wiki/Alvaro_Mendoza "Alvaro Mendoza") entered the Guarne area, but finding no [gold](/wiki/Gold "Gold"), returned to the [Aburrá Valley](/wiki/Aburr%C3%A1_Valley "Aburrá Valley"). In 1640, searching for gold, Captain [Fernando del Toro Zapata](/w/index.php?title=Fernando_del_Toro_Zapata&action=edit&redlink=1 "Fernando del Toro Zapata (page does not exist)") and [Diego Beltran del Castillo](/w/index.php?title=Diego_Beltran_del_Castillo&action=edit&redlink=1 "Diego Beltran del Castillo (page does not exist)") arrived at Guarne. The first non-Amerindian settlers of the area of Guarne were [slaves](/wiki/Slaves "Slaves"). In 1757, Guarne became part of [Rionegro](/wiki/Rionegro "Rionegro"), and in 1814, Juan del Corral created the Guarne Township.
